title: Nonreciprocal magnon-magnon entanglement in a spinning cavity-magnon system
authors: Zhisheng Xu, Mengxue Li, Chunfang Sun, Gangcheng Wang
abstract: Cavity-magnon systems, combining magnons and photons, offer a versatile platform for studying quantum entanglement and advancing quantum information science. In this work, we propose a scheme for generating nonreciprocal magnon-magnon entanglement in a hybrid system consisting of two yttrium iron garnet spheres coupled to a spinning whispering-gallery-mode cavity. By leveraging the magnon Kerr nonlinearity and the Sagnac effect arising from the cavity rotation, we show that the entanglement can be substantially enhanced, and the resulting entanglement exhibits pronounced nonreciprocal characteristics. Furthermore, our scheme demonstrates that the entanglement remains robust against thermal noise and persists at bath temperatures up to 100 mK. This work underscores the potential of spinning cavity-magnon systems as a versatile platform for realizing nonreciprocal quantum devices and facilitating the development of quantum technologies.
summary: This paper proposes a method to create directional (nonreciprocal) entanglement between two magnon modes in a spinning cavity system. By leveraging the Sagnac effect from cavity rotation and the Kerr nonlinearity of magnons, the researchers show that entanglement can be precisely controlled and remains stable even at relatively high temperatures.
main_problem: The generation and control of nonreciprocal magnon-magnon entanglement in a hybrid cavity-magnon system using the Sagnac and Kerr effects.
main_result: The study demonstrates that magnon-magnon entanglement can be nonreciprocally enhanced or suppressed by tuning the Sagnac-Fizeau shift and the magnon Kerr nonlinearity, with entanglement remaining robust up to temperatures of 100 mK.
method: The authors use Quantum Langevin Equations and a linearized effective Hamiltonian, applying a joint unitary squeezing transformation and solving the Lyapunov equation to quantify entanglement via logarithmic negativity.
model_or_system: A hybrid system consisting of two Yttrium Iron Garnet (YIG) spheres coupled to a spinning whispering-gallery-mode (WGM) cavity, where the cavity rotation induces a Sagnac-Fizeau shift and the magnons exhibit Kerr nonlinearity.
key_observables: Magnon-magnon entanglement (logarithmic negativity), average photon number, and average magnon numbers.
important_parameters: Sagnac-Fizeau frequency shift (Delta_F), magnon Kerr coefficient (K_j), coupling strength (g_mj,a), decay rates (kappa), and bath temperature (T).
main_assumptions: The system is in a strong driving regime allowing for linearization, the Markovian approximation is used for input noise, and the relativistic dispersion term in the Sagnac effect is neglected.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the system schematic and coupling configuration; Fig 3-5 show entanglement dependence on Sagnac/Kerr shifts and detuning; Fig 6-8 show the effects of coupling strength and decay rates; Fig 9 shows the survival of entanglement with temperature.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the hybrid system, defines the Hamiltonian and linearized dynamics, derives the resonance condition via a squeezing transformation, analyzes the impact of various physical parameters on entanglement, and concludes with the robustness of the system against thermal noise.
why_interesting: It provides a theoretical framework for using mechanical rotation (Sagnac effect) and nonlinearities to engineer directional quantum correlations, which is highly relevant for developing nonreciprocal quantum networks and devices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic of the spinning WGM cavity-magnon sys- tem. K j is the magnon Kerr coefficient, which can be adjusted by the direction of the magnetic field Bj. When the magnetic field is aligned along the crystal axis, K j > 0; otherwise, K j < 0. For the spinning cavity, when the driving field is clockwise (counterclock- wise), a positive (negative) frequency shift ∆F > 0 (< 0) is generated via the Sagnac effect. (b) The coupling configuration. Kerr magnons with decay rates κmj are coupled to photons in a spinning cavity with decay rate κa. The corresponding coupling strength is gmja.

FIG. 4. The effect of normalized cavity field frequency detuning ∆a/2π on magnon-magnon entanglement Em1m2 N . Parameter settings: ∆m1/2π = −10 MHz, ∆m2/2π = 10 MHz, ∆F/2π = ±1 and 0 MHz, (a) ∆K1 = ∆K2 = 1 MHz; (b) ∆K1 = ∆K2 = −1 MHz. Other parame- ters are the same as those in Fig. 2.

FIG. 2. The relationship between the magnon-magnon entanglement Em1m2 N and the normalized magnon frequency detuning ∆mj/2π with (a) ∆K1 = ∆K2 > 0, ∆F > 0; (b) ∆K1 = ∆K2 > 0, ∆F = 0; (c) ∆K1 = ∆K2 > 0, ∆F < 0; (d) ∆K1 = ∆K2 < 0, ∆F > 0; (e) ∆K1 = ∆K2 < 0, ∆F = 0; (f) ∆K1 = ∆K2 < 0, ∆F < 0.

FIG. 3. The impact of the normalized Kerr frequency shift ∆K/2π and Sagnac frequency shift ∆F/2π on the magnon-magnon entan- glement Em1m2 N is shown with (a) ∆F/2π = ±1 and 0 MHz; (b) ∆K/2π = ±1 MHz, where ∆m1/2π = −10 MHz, ∆m2/2π = 10 MHz, and other parameters are the same as those in Fig. 2.

FIG. 6. The effect of the normalized magnon-photon coupling strength gma/2π on the magnon-magnon entanglement Em1m2 N with (a) ∆K1 = ∆K2 = 1 MHz; (b) ∆K1 = ∆K2 = −1 MHz, where ∆m1/2π = −10 MHz, ∆m2/2π = 10 MHz, ∆F/2π = ±1 and 0 MHz, and other parameters are the same as those in Fig. 2.

Spintronics.note: The paper is highly relevant as it directly studies the quantum-optical control of magnon-magnon entanglement in a hybrid cavity-magnon system using Kerr nonlinearity and the Sagnac effect.
